Patch Set 2: I would prefer that you didn't submit this

(2 inline comments)

Marking -1 until I understand the network protocol implications better.

Does the client hard-code the /.lustre/fid/ FID, or will it be refreshed if the server is upgraded (while there are mounted clients) from 2.1 to 2.4?  Will the client drop this FID from its local cache after an hour or two?

If there is only a potential problem of lustre_rsync not working for a short time after an upgrade, maybe this isn't a terrible bug.  If the clients are all unable to use /.lustre/fid permanently after a server is upgraded, then it might be a more serious problem.

Patch Set 2: (2 inline comments)

Currently, the LFSCK for linkEA is processed in MDD layer, FID-in-dirent and OI mapping are processed in OSD layer. For MDT layer, it knows nothing about the LFSCK processing. But related LDLM lock are handled in MDT layer. That means, the client may hold related LDLM locks on the ".lustre", ".lustre/obf" object when LFSCK check/repair them, but the LFSCK cannot revoke related LDLM locks, so the client cannot detect the changes caused by LFSCK. So here, we should not change OBF fid as did in my patch. I will consider other solutions.

Thanks for pointing the issues out.

(9 inline comments)

Some changes that I think would improve the code readability could be made in separate patches, since they are not necessarily directly related to this patch.

Some minor cosmetic fixes (tabs, spaces) if patch is refreshed.

Mike, I'd really appreciate your input on this patch.

Patch Set 3:

just to make my point of view clear: hint is an optimization, in some case it can be introduced to improve performance choosing "faster" path, like we do with NEW

Patch Set 3: I would prefer that you didn't submit this

sorry.. got wrong keystroke pressed.

just to make my point of view clear: hint is an optimization, in some case it can be introduced to improve performance choosing "faster" path, like we do with NEW. 

but you say "OSD can distinguish special objects by checking those well-known FIDs one by one, but it is inconvenient and inefficient". which means to me that we do no look at FID, instead we look at the hint. which is totally wrong. in OSD the FID only matters. only FIDs make objects unique.

parents.subject: LU-1866 misc: fix some issues found during LFSCK

commit.subject: LU-1866 fid: cleanup object visibility definition and check
commit.message:
LU-1866 fid: cleanup object visibility definition and check

1) The normal fid, IGIF fid, "ROOT" fid, ".lustre" fid are
   used for client-mdt visible objects.

2) Remove redundan osd_igif.{c,h}

3) Hide server-side local root for up layer dir readpage.

4) It is unnecessary to append FID-in-dient for dot name entry.
   Because we can get it from the directory object directly.

Signed-off-by: Fan Yong <fan.yong@intel.com>
Change-Id: If98de0c15c0cd3a0e93b666a8d48366b0d8a2cb0
